company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: James Li - Assistant Professor of Emergency Medicine at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: James Li, MD, MEd, FAEMS is an Assistant Professor of Emergency Medicine at Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis. He completed his undergraduate degree at Emory University in Atlanta, then earned his MD at Drexel University College of Medicine. He completed emergency medicine residency, served as chief resident, completed an EMS fellowship, and earned a Master of Education at the University of Cincinnati. At WashU Medicine, he serves as Associate Program Director for the EMS Fellowship and director for emergency medicine resident EMS rotations. He is also medical director for multiple EMS agencies and fire departments in the St. Louis region.

company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: Namrata Patel - Assistant Professor of Medicine and Pediatrics at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: Namrata Patel, MD is an Assistant Professor of Medicine and Pediatrics at Washington University School of Medicine, where she has been faculty since 2019. She completed her undergraduate studies at Emory University, earning a Bachelor of Science in Biology. She then earned her MD at The University of Tennessee College of Medicine, followed by a combined internal medicine/pediatrics residency at Case Western Reserve University. She serves as director of the Future Workforce in Medicine program and founded the DOM Parents and Caregivers subcommittee in 2020. She has a strong focus on professional development for trainees and faculty.

_enrichment.title: Assistant Professor of Medicine and Pediatrics

company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: Matthew Glasser - Faculty, Mallinckrodt Institute of Radiology at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: Matthew Glasser, MD, PhD is a neuroradiology faculty member at the Mallinckrodt Institute of Radiology at Washington University School of Medicine. He earned his Bachelor of Science in Neuroscience from Emory University, completing an honors thesis in the lab of James Rilling on myeloarchitecture MRI mapping. He later completed post-baccalaureate premedical studies at Johns Hopkins before joining WashU in 2008, where he earned both his MD and PhD in Neuroscience. He is best known for mapping the human brain's neocortical areas as part of the Human Connectome Project and co-leads a brain imaging laboratory with David Van Essen in the Department of Neuroscience.

company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: Javaughn Baker - Anesthesiology Resident at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: Javaughn Baker, MD is a resident physician in the Department of Anesthesiology at Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis, originally from Panama City, Florida. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Chemistry from Emory University in 2015, then a Master of Health Science in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology from Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health in 2016. He completed his MD at Meharry Medical College, one of four historically Black medical schools in the US. His interest in anesthesiology was sparked by participation in the FAER Medical Student Anesthesia Research Program during medical school. He went on to fellowship in Adult Cardiothoracic Anesthesiology at WashU.

company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: Avi Dobrusin - Chief Anesthesiology Resident at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: Avi Dobrusin, MD was a CA-3 and Chief Resident in the Department of Anesthesiology at Washington University School of Medicine, completing his residency in 2024. He earned his Bachelor of Science in Anthropology and Human Biology from Emory University, then worked as an EMT in Atlanta before attending Central Michigan University College of Medicine for his MD. He received the Fabian Award for Outstanding First Year Resident at WashU in 2022. He went on to fellowship training in cardiothoracic anesthesiology and critical care medicine with a career focus on academic anesthesiology and medical education. He is originally from Ann Arbor, Michigan.

_enrichment.title: Anesthesiology Resident (Chief Resident) / Cardiothoracic Anesthesiology Fellow

company_name: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
suggested_title: Tamanna Chang - Pediatric Anesthesiology Fellow at WashU Medicine
prospect_notes: Tamanna Chang, MD is a Pediatric Anesthesiology fellow (Class of 2022) at Washington University School of Medicine, having stayed on after completing her Anesthesiology residency at WashU. She attended Emory University in Atlanta, where she majored in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology, then earned her MD at Meharry Medical College in Nashville. Born in Kerman, Iran and raised in the Bronx, NY before her family moved to Nashville, she served as social/wellness chief for the WashU anesthesiology residency program. She is passionate about global health and previously provided medical care in Costa Rica, Nicaragua, and Bangladesh. After WashU, she went on to faculty at Vanderbilt University Medical Center.
